exercises.title: Hamming Code Constructor
exercises.description: Build the parity check and generator matrices for Hamming codes.
tasks: Construct H matrix with columns as binary numbers 1 to n
Derive systematic form generator matrix G
Verify G × H^T = 0 (mod 2)
Generalize to any value of r
Display matrices for [7,4,3] and [15,11,3] codes
exercises.expectedOutput: Correct H and G matrices with verification
hints: Columns of H are binary representations of 1 to 2^r - 1
G = [I_k | P] where P comes from H
All products should be zero vectors

exercises.title: Syndrome Decoder
exercises.description: Implement complete syndrome decoding for Hamming codes.
tasks: Calculate syndrome s = H × r^T
Convert syndrome to error position
Correct single-bit errors
Extract message from corrected codeword
Test on all possible single-error patterns
exercises.expectedOutput: Working syndrome decoder with 100% correction rate for single errors
hints: Binary value of syndrome = error position
XOR received word with error pattern to correct

exercises.title: Extended Hamming Implementation
exercises.description: Implement the extended Hamming code with distance 4.
tasks: Add overall parity bit to encoding
Detect vs correct based on syndrome + parity
Handle all 4 cases: no error, 1 error, 2 errors, parity error
Compare with regular Hamming code
Demonstrate 2-error detection
exercises.expectedOutput: Extended Hamming code distinguishing 1 vs 2 errors
hints: Overall parity = XOR of all bits

exercises.title: Hamming vs Repetition Comparison
exercises.description: Compare efficiency of Hamming and repetition codes.
tasks: Calculate error probability after decoding for both codes
Compare code rates
Plot error probability vs physical error rate
Find crossover points where one is better
Recommend best code for given constraints
hints: Hamming: n=7, k=4; Rep-3: n=3, k=1
Hamming has higher rate but also higher failure prob per bit
